title: What planning a feature produces
question: When the AI consults the log for a feature, does it write anything, or just advise?
rationale: Consulting is advisory and writes nothing: the plan flow briefs (related decisions, tradeoffs, recommended fit, risks) and stops. A decision enters the log only if the user then decides to build it, through normal confirm-at-the-moment — keeping the rule never to log before it's agreed.

options.label: Briefing first, capture only if decided

tradeoffs.note: writes nothing until the user actually decides
still hands off cleanly to the normal add flow when a decision is reached
the AI must know when advising ends and capturing begins
the feature is recorded in one step
writes before the decision is actually made
artificially refuses to log even once the decision is made
